asset.title: Stack Before After V1
asset.purpose: Narrated explainer episode. Judge story clarity, visual design, proof quality, and whether it fits the README claim.

judge.summary: A clear before-and-after comparison demonstrating the transition from a single-player Streamlit workflow to a collaborative NodeRoom environment with multi-player state and agent reviews.

featureClarity.evidence: Demonstrates the contrast between single-player Streamlit and multi-player NodeRoom.

workflowCompleteness.evidence: Shows the initial Streamlit run, explains the limitation, and demonstrates the NodeRoom solution.

visualDesign.evidence: Clean UI presentation, though the text slides are somewhat basic.

consistency.evidence: Aligns well with NodeRoom's core concepts of shared spreadsheets and agent collaboration.

evidenceQuality.evidence: Features actual product UI captures alongside explanatory slides.

legibility.evidence: UI text and spreadsheet cells are generally readable, though small in some areas.

professionalRelevance.evidence: Highly relevant for teams looking to build collaborative agentic workflows.

productionHonesty.evidence: Clearly distinguishes between the mock/Streamlit phase and the live NodeRoom environment.

observedEvidence: NodeRoom Spreadsheet collaboration and agent proposal UI

defects.observed: The transition slides interrupt the product flow with plain text.
defects.fix: Integrate the explanatory text as voiceover or overlay on top of the active UI.

judge.suggestedReadmeCaption: See how NodeRoom upgrades a standard single-player Streamlit pipeline into a live, multi-player collaborative workspace with built-in agent review lanes.
